transcript.text: Hi there. This is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show. Two classic sessions tonight from Jethro Tull. The first recorded in November 1968, the second in June 1969. We've also got the latest session by the Tom Robinson Band.

You'll hear classic cuts by Genesis, Judas Priest and Peter Frampton among others, and new cuts by Journey, Ian Hunter and Van Halen. Plus, the Friday Night Connection, an 18-minute segue of magic that's a must for headphone freaks.
But tonight, we start with Motörhead.
That's a new single that's just come out by UFO on Chrysalis, Shoot Shoot, taken from their live album.
We were checking in the records today, though, and we found that the song was first found on Fawcett, an album that came out in 1975.
And prior to that, you heard Motorhead with a track from their album, which is called Overkill, as indeed was that cut.
And we played it because David Wall wanted us to do so.
David lives in Forest Lodge, Yarwell Road, Wansford, I think it is, in Peterborough.

who make that wall of sound in Leicester sometime in April.
